keyPoints: Atomic Commit Protocol ensures that either all or none of the transactions in a distributed transaction are committed.
Concurrency control prevents modifications to shared data by multiple transactions.
Distributed deadlocks can occur when two or more transactions wait for each other to release resources.
Transaction recovery is the process of restoring the system to a consistent state after a failure or crash.
The 2PC protocol ensures that either all or none of the transactions in a distributed transaction are committed.
